options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: Itchy_And_Scratchy_Ticket_Requirement
options.description:
Goal is watching the Itchy and Scratchy film in the Lobby.
You'll receive the Ticket when you complete the requirements chosen.

options.name: Wasp_Amount
options.description: Number of Wasps additionally required to gain the Itchy and Scratchy ticket. There are 20 per level.

options.name: Card_Amount
options.description: Number of Cards additionally required to gain the Itchy and Scratchy ticket. There are 7 per level.

options.name: Required_Mission_Levels
options.description:
If the ticket requires missions or story missions, choose which level's missions are required.
Missions in other levels may still unlock required items.
Has no effect if the ticket doesn't require missions.
Valid options are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, or All.

options.name: Car_Amount
options.description: If the ticket requires cars collected, choose the amount of cars required.

options.name: Lock_Levels
options.description: If enabled, levels are will be inaccessible to Free Roam until you receive their respective Level item.

options.name: Early_Level
options.description: If enabled, attempt to place at least one of your required levels in an early location.

options.name: Shuffle_Levels
options.description:
Choose how you want Levels to be received.
If shuffled, 6 Level X items will be shuffled into the item pool which will allow access to that level's missions
Otherwise, 6 Progressive Level items will be shuffled into the item pool, allowing you to access levels in the vanilla order.
Regardless of your choice, missions can be played in any order on an unlocked level.

options.name: Starting_Car_Shuffle
options.description:
Choose whether to shuffle starting car between available cars from your starting level.
If disabled, you'll always start with your starting level's default car.

options.name: Shuffle_Attack
options.description:
Choose whether to add Attack to the pool for each character.
Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.

options.name: Shuffle_Jump
options.description:
Choose whether to add Jump to the pool for each character.
Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.

options.name: Start_Jump_Level
options.description:
Choose how many Progressive Jump items to start with for each character.
Characters who aren't included in ShuffleJump will start with Progressive Jump Level 2.

options.name: Shuffle_Gagfinder
options.description:
Choose whether to add a Gagfinder to the pool for each Character that will be required to
unlock gags as that character. Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.
If None, gags will instead be locked until you receive their Level.

options.name: Shuffle_Checkered_Flags
options.description:
Choose whether to add a Checkered Flag to the pool for each Character that will be required to
unlock races as that character. Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.
If None, races will instead be locked until you receive their Level.

options.name: Shuffle_EBrakes
options.description:
Choose whether to shuffle ability to use the E-Brake for each character into the item pool.
Characters not chosen will always be able to use the E-Brake.
Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.

options.name: Shuffle_Bumpers
options.description:
Choose whether to shuffle Frink-o-Matic Wasp Bumpers into the item pool.
Characters not chosen won't have a Wasp Bumper in the item pool and *WILL NOT* be able to break wasps with cars.
Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.

options.name: Start_With_Bumpers
options.description:
If true, add all enabled Wasp Bumpers to the start inventory.
If you want more granular control of this, use the generic start inventory.

options.name: Shuffle_Forward
options.description:
Choose whether to shuffle the ability to move forward or drive forward for each character into the item pool.
Characters not chosen won't have a forward in the item pool and will always be able to move forward.
Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None.
**THIS COULD CREATE VERY DIFFICULT SEEDS**

options.name: Early_Forward
options.description:
If enabled and forward is shuffled, try to place each specified character's Forward item early.
Valid options are Homer, Bart, Lisa, Marge, Apu, All, or None. Does nothing if the character's forward isn't shuffled.

options.name: Shuffle_Cards
options.description:
Randomize card locations. This option adds several possible locations for
cards. There will still be 49 total cards with 7 in each level.

options.name: Mission_Locks
options.description: Add car requirements to a percentage of missions

options.name: Mission_Timers
options.description:
Increase mission timers by the chosen percentage.
For example, if a mission has a timer of 1:00 and the chosen value is 50, then the mission will have a timer of 1:30

options.name: Min_Shop_Price
options.description: The minimum cost of any item in Gil's Shop. If this is greater than the max shop price, then the max will be used instead.

options.name: Max_Shop_Price
options.description: The maximum cost of any item in Gil's Shop.

options.name: Shop_Scale_Modifier
options.description:
The multiplier for shop costs per levels. For each level after L1, min and max will be multiplied by level number and this multiplier.
L2 = Min/Max * Mod * 2, L3 = Min/Max * Mod * 3, etc.

options.name: Start_Wallet_Level
options.description:
Choose how many Progressive Wallet Levels to start with.
Level 1 shops are in logic with 1 level, level 2 with 2, etc.
Your coin cap = MaxShopPrice * WalletLevel * ShopScaleMod and is unlimited at Wallet Level 7

options.name: Shop_Hint_Policy
options.description:
Choose the level of hints sent when speaking to Gil for the first time on a level
All: Hint all items in Gil's shop
OnlyProg: Only hint progression items in Gil's shop
None: No shop hints will be created

options.name: Extra_Hint_Policy
options.description: Place extra hints in various places.

options.name: Filler_Wrench_Efficiency
options.description: Percentage damage a filler wrench item will repair your cars.

options.name: Filler_HitNRun_Reset_Efficiency
options.display_name: Filler Hit N Run Reset Efficiency
options.description: Percentage a filler Hit N Run Reset item will lower your Hit N Run meter.

options.name: Shuffle_Traffic
options.description: Randomize traffic per level. Cars that are usually used as traffic in that level plus the starting car of the next level are unable to be chosen for their level.

options.name: Traffic_Blacklist
options.description:
Block cars from appearing as traffic. There must be at least 35 cars available to shuffle.
If there are less than 5 cars available for any level after applying the blacklist, then traffic will not be shuffled.

options.name: Filler_Traps
options.description:
How many fillers will be replaced with traps. 0 means no additional traps, 100 means all fillers are traps.
These traps are IN ADDITION to any enabled traps above, though it won't add trap types that have been disabled.

options.name: Enable_Eject_Traps
options.description: Whether to include Eject traps in the item pool.

options.name: Enable_Duff_Traps
options.description: Whether to include Duff traps in the item pool.

options.name: Enable_Launch_Traps
options.description: Whether to include Launch traps in the item pool.

options.name: Enable_HitNRun_Traps
options.description: Whether to include Hit N Run traps in the item pool.

options.name: Enable_Traffic_Traps
options.description: Whether to include Traffic traps in the item pool.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
